title: euphorbia garuana
intro: Euphorbia garuana, also known as the Candelabra tree or St. John's Spurge, is a species of succulent plant in the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Euphorbia garuana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's milky sap is often used to create ointments, creams, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
